layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-REVISED
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH MULTI-ROW WORKSTREAM TABLE LAYOUT. The slide is dominated by a single large table (C1) that spans nearly the entire slide width and height, with a compact title/header area (C0) positioned above it. The layout uses a top-to-bottom orientation with minimal padding to accommodate the dense tabular content. The table is structured as a multi-column grid with 4 time-period columns (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) plus 3 metadata columns (Workstream, Details, Owner(s)) on the left, creating a 7-column structure. CRITICAL STRUCTURAL CORRECTION: The table contains 11 distinct data rows, not 5. Each unique combination of Details + Owner(s) in the source slide is rendered as a separate table row. Workstream 'i. Conduct discovery' spans 3 rows (one per Details entry), 'ii. Select and onboard partner' spans 3 rows, and the remaining workstreams each span 1 row. This creates an 11-row × 7-column grid (77 cells total). The design follows the Flat Geometry Table pattern (Section 4 Option C) appropriate for formal project planning content: no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey row label cells in the Workstream column, and thin dashed grey separator lines between rows to create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. Row heights are content-weighted: rows with dense timeline deliverables (6–10 bullet points) receive significantly more vertical space than rows with sparse deliverables (1–3 bullets). All content is distributed evenly across the available slide area with minimal whitespace, reflecting the high information density of this project workplan. Horizontal alignment is absolute: every row band's top and bottom edges align precisely across all seven columns.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.08, 0.92]

Containers:
  C0 (TITLE AND TIMELINE HEADER AREA): Compact header zone occupying approximately 8% of slide height at the top. Contains the slide title 'Fund Accounting platform initiative workplan' on the left side and a timeline header 'Deliverables against each workstream' spanning the right portion. The title uses heading font in brand primary color, medium size. The timeline header sits aligned with the timeline columns below it, using body font in brand text color, slightly smaller than the title. A 'Current focus' label appears in the top-right corner as a small text element in body font. The yellow annotation box from the source slide is removed entirely (editorial comment, not content). This area establishes the slide context and aligns horizontally with the table structure below. Minimal vertical padding between this header and the table top edge — approximately 1% of slide height to visually separate title from content without wasting space. The header text is left-aligned with the table's left edge. No background fill, no border.
  C1 (FULL-WIDTH MULTI-ROW WORKSTREAM TABLE): Dominant container occupying approximately 88% of slide height and 96% of slide width, positioned directly below C0 with minimal gap. Structured as a formal project workplan table using the Flat Geometry Table pattern (Design Guidelines Section 4 Option C). Seven columns total: three metadata columns on the left (Workstream, Details, Owner(s)) and four timeline columns on the right (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027). CRITICAL STRUCTURE: 11 data rows, not 5. Each unique Details + Owner(s) combination from the source slide is rendered as a separate table row. Row grouping: Workstream 'i. Conduct discovery' spans rows 1–3 (Details: 'Define high-level business requirements', 'Design target state architecture', 'Design target state data flows'), 'ii. Select and onboard partner' spans rows 4–6 (Details: 'Evaluate and select fund accounting platform partner', 'Assess if 3rd-party consulting support is required', 'Negotiate and finalize contract(s)'), 'iii. Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC' is row 7, 'iv. Implement fund accounting platform - Holdings' is row 8, 'v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments' is row 9. Rows 10–11 are additional sub-rows if present in source data (verify exact count from source content). COLUMN HEADERS: Plain bold text in body font (10pt), brand text color, left-aligned within each column with small left inset. Below each header: a small accent tab (brand primary color, 2pt height, width approximately 15% of column width, top corners slightly rounded with 2pt radius, left-aligned beneath the header text, sitting fully below text with 1pt gap). Immediately below and touching each accent tab: a thin solid horizontal line (1pt weight, same brand primary color) spanning that column's width with 2% white gutters between columns (line does not span full table width, each column has its own segment). COLUMN WIDTHS: Workstream column 18% of table width (sized to fit longest workstream name 'v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments' with wrapping to 2–3 lines). Details column 20% of table width (widest metadata column to accommodate longer descriptions like 'Configure and test the system, conduct parallel runs and go-live on the new platform'). Owner(s) column 14% of table width. Timeline columns (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) share remaining 48% equally, each 12% of table width. ROW HEIGHTS: Content-weighted using proportional flexGrow. Rows with dense timeline deliverables (6–10 bullets, e.g., row 1 'Define high-level business requirements' with 3 bullets in Q2, row 4 'Evaluate and select fund accounting platform partner' with 4 bullets in Q2 and 2 bullets in Q3) receive flexGrow values of 3–4. Rows with sparse deliverables (1–3 bullets, e.g., row 7 'Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC' with 1 bullet in Q4 and 2 bullets in 2027) receive flexGrow values of 1.5–2. This ensures taller rows for dense content and prevents text overflow. Total flexGrow sum across all 11 rows distributes the 88% vertical space proportionally. WORKSTREAM COLUMN CELLS: Light grey filled rectangles (fill: light grey alt, no border, no rounded corners, full column width, occupying 90% of row height with 5% top/bottom margin within row band) with bold text in brand text color, left-aligned with 3% left inset, vertically centered. The workstream label (e.g., 'i. Conduct discovery') appears only in the FIRST row of each workstream group. Subsequent rows within the same workstream group have an empty Workstream cell (transparent, no fill, no text) to visually indicate continuation of the same workstream. Text wraps to multiple lines where needed. DETAILS COLUMN CELLS: Plain text on white background, body font regular weight (9pt to fit dense text), brand text color, left-aligned with 2% left inset, vertically centered within row band. Each row contains one Details entry from the source slide (e.g., row 1: 'Define high-level business requirements', row 2: 'Design target state architecture', row 3: 'Design target state data flows'). Text wraps as needed to fit within column width. OWNER(S) COLUMN CELLS: Plain text on white background, body font regular weight (9pt), brand text color, left-aligned with 2% left inset, vertically centered. Department/role labels (Finance/Ops:, Tech:, TO:) in brand text color followed by names in regular weight. Each row has its own Owner(s) assignment matching the source slide structure. Text wraps to multiple lines where needed (e.g., 'Finance/Ops: Katarina (with input from Tyson, Moira/George, Tom, Therese, Patrick, Tiffany)' wraps to 3–4 lines). TIMELINE COLUMN CELLS (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027): Bullet lists using native PowerPoint bullets (small brand-color circles, 4pt diameter), body font regular weight (9pt to accommodate dense content), brand text color, left-aligned with 4% left inset to accommodate bullets. Each cell contains the deliverable items from the source slide as separate bullet points. Bullets are vertically distributed within the row band using equal spacing (flexbox column with space-between). Text wraps within cell boundaries. Font size reduced to 9pt to prevent overflow in dense cells (e.g., row 1 Q2 cell has 3 bullets: 'Draft high-level business requirements', 'Prioritize requirements by must-have and nice-to-have', 'Align on requirements and prioritization'). All bullet text must be fully visible and readable with no overlap or clipping. ROW SEPARATORS: Thin dashed grey horizontal lines (1pt weight, light grey color, 3pt dash / 2pt gap pattern) between consecutive data rows, spanning full table width from left edge to right edge. Positioned at the exact boundary between row bands (no gap above or below the separator — it sits tight to the row edges on dense slides per Beauty Principle 0.1). Separator after row 3 (end of 'i. Conduct discovery' group), after row 6 (end of 'ii. Select and onboard partner' group), after row 7, after row 8, after row 9, after row 10. No separator after the final row (row 11). These separators are critical for visual rhythm given the minimal padding between rows. HORIZONTAL ALIGNMENT ENFORCEMENT: Each of the 11 data rows is implemented as a single flexbox row container with 7 child cells (one per column). All cells in a row share identical top_pct and height_pct values. This ensures absolute horizontal alignment across all seven columns — every row band's top and bottom edges align precisely across the full table width. No independent vertical positioning per column is permitted. VISUAL TREATMENT: No cell borders except the column header underlines. No alternating row fills — the light grey workstream column cells provide the only background color variation. All text must fit within cell boundaries at the specified font sizes (9pt body for table cells, 10pt bold for headers). The table fills the available vertical space completely, with row heights proportional to content volume as described above. The table has no outer border or background fill — it sits directly on the white slide background.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide is fully occupied by the title area and the large workstream table. No decorative background layer is needed — the table structure itself provides sufficient visual organization and the content density leaves no empty space requiring fill. Adding a background layer would violate the accent color budget (Rule C3) and add no grouping benefit. The table sits directly on the white slide background.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide contains no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. The workstream table is self-contained with no visual relationships requiring top-layer rendering. All content sits within the table structure. No connectors span between containers.
